like-patterns.label: Pattern Matching with LIKE
like-patterns.summary: Search for patterns in text using LIKE and wildcards.

sketch-like-intro.title: Introduction to LIKE
sketch-like-intro.bodyMarkdown: The `LIKE` operator in SQL allows you to search for patterns within text columns. You can use wildcards such as `%` (any sequence of characters) and `_` (a single character) to match flexible patterns in your queries.

sketch-like-wildcards.title: Wildcards in LIKE
sketch-like-wildcards.bodyMarkdown: The `%` wildcard matches zero or more characters, while `_` matches exactly one character. For example, `name LIKE 'A%'` finds names starting with 'A', and `code LIKE 'A_1'` matches any code where the first character is 'A', followed by any single character, and ending with '1'.

quiz-like-basic-single.title: Basic LIKE Pattern
quiz-like-basic-single.prompt: Which SQL query finds all products whose names start with 'Pro' in the products_catalog table?
quiz-like-basic-single.hint: Remember how the % wildcard works at the end of a pattern.

help.concept: The % wildcard matches any sequence of characters, including none. Placing it at the end of a string matches any text that starts with the given prefix.
help.hint_1: To match names that start with 'Pro', the pattern should begin with 'Pro' and end with a wildcard.
help.hint_2: The correct pattern is 'Pro%' to match any name that starts with 'Pro'.

options.a: SELECT * FROM products_catalog WHERE name LIKE 'Pro%';
options.b: SELECT * FROM products_catalog WHERE name LIKE '%Pro';
options.c: SELECT * FROM products_catalog WHERE name LIKE '%Pro%';
options.d: SELECT * FROM products_catalog WHERE name LIKE '_Pro%';

quiz-like-wildcard-multi.title: Using Wildcards with LIKE
quiz-like-wildcard-multi.prompt: Which of the following patterns will match the product name 'ProMax'?
quiz-like-wildcard-multi.hint: Think about where the wildcards are placed in each pattern.

help.concept: LIKE patterns can match text at the start, end, or anywhere in the string depending on the placement of % or _ wildcards.
help.hint_1: Patterns with % at the start or end can match substrings at different positions.
help.hint_2: Check which patterns could match 'ProMax' at the start, end, or in the middle.

quiz-like-drag-order.title: Order the Steps: Using LIKE
quiz-like-drag-order.prompt: Arrange the steps to write a query that finds all customers whose email ends with '@gmail.com' in the customers_cleanup table.
quiz-like-drag-order.hint: Start with the SELECT statement, then add the WHERE clause, and finally the pattern.

help.concept: A SQL query typically starts with SELECT, followed by FROM, WHERE, and then the condition or pattern.
help.hint_1: The WHERE clause comes after the table selection.
help.hint_2: The LIKE pattern should be the last part of the WHERE clause.

quiz-like-fill-blank.title: Fill in the Blank: LIKE Pattern
quiz-like-fill-blank.prompt: Fill in the blank to match any product code that starts with 'A' and is exactly 3 characters long: `code LIKE '___'`
quiz-like-fill-blank.hint: You need a pattern that starts with 'A' and has two more characters.

help.concept: The _ wildcard matches a single character. To match exactly three characters starting with 'A', use 'A__'.
help.hint_2: Combine 'A' with two underscores to make a three-character pattern.

quiz-like-code-input.title: Write a LIKE Query
quiz-like-code-input.prompt: Write a SQL query to select all products from products_catalog whose description contains the word 'eco' anywhere in the text.
quiz-like-code-input.hint: Use the % wildcard before and after 'eco' to match it anywhere.

help.concept: To match a substring anywhere in a text column, use % on both sides of the pattern in LIKE.
help.hint_1: The pattern should be '%eco%' to find 'eco' anywhere in the description.
help.hint_2: Place % before and after 'eco' in your LIKE condition.
